placeholder_ergonomics.reason: Bare underscore-delimited placeholders are double-click selectable without punctuation cleanup.

prompt_library.prompt_tab_color_match: exact semantic row fill RGB where Excel supports RGB tab colors

prompt_body_range.description: Every prompt tab exposes one deterministic interior range between its top and bottom navigation rows.

scaffold_fill.description: Neutral configurable scaffold to prove complete interior body-range ownership without claiming a final dark palette.

range_rules.top_nav_row: lowest row containing a HYPERLINK formula whose location references Prompt_Library
range_rules.bottom_nav_row: highest row containing a HYPERLINK formula whose location references Prompt_Library
range_rules.columns: all columns present in the sheet's dimension

technician_view_profile.description: Hidden-column profile that preserves developer-facing data while reducing technician-facing visual noise.
technician_view_profile.hidden_columns_note: Default is empty (show all columns). The operator-modified workbook contains the preferred set; inspect it to populate this profile. Columns are hidden never deleted.
technician_view_profile.preservation_rule: Hidden columns remain addressable by formulas links agents and developer tooling. Column data must never be deleted.

dark_theme.description: Accessible dark rendition for Excel-for-the-web interfaces. Canvas and content surfaces are darkened coherently with WCAG AA contrast compliance.

canvas.description: Primary canvas background and default text for dark rendition.

surface.description: Raised surface fill for non-canvas sheet areas.

link.description: Hyperlink color in dark mode. Must remain visually distinct from body text.

navigation.description: Navigation row fill and text for top/bottom prompt-tab navigation.

border.description: Separator and border color for dark surfaces.

scaffold_fill.description: Dark variant of the neutral scaffold fill for body-range ownership proof.

contrast_gates.description: WCAG AA contrast thresholds. Fail closed when any registered foreground/background pair misses its required threshold.
